content:
We need to verify that the GitHub repository contains the latest actual code and is fully in sync with the local project.

Repository:
https://github.com/deadevil2002/heavyar-app.git

Local project path:
C:\Heavyar_Last\heavyar-app-Last

Actual app location:
C:\Heavyar_Last\heavyar-app-Last\expo

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Do NOT modify any code
- Do NOT create or refactor anything
- Do NOT run any dev server
- Read and compare only

Task:

1. Pull the latest version from GitHub
2. Compare it with the local project at:
   C:\Heavyar_Last\heavyar-app-Last\expo

3. Verify file-by-file:
   - app/
   - components/
   - services/
   - contexts/
   - types/
   - i18n/

4. Specifically verify the latest feature:
Advanced Rental Request Flow

Check if the following exist and match between local and GitHub:

- RequestMode
- numberOfDays
- RentalRequestModal
- fixed duration flow
- open-ended request flow
- createRequest input structure
- parseRequest updates
- request-related types
- request mocks

For each item, return:
- MATCH (same in GitHub and local)
- DIFFERENT (explain difference)
- MISSING IN GITHUB
- MISSING LOCALLY

5. Check commit status:
- What is the latest commit hash on GitHub?
- What is the latest local commit hash?
- Are there uncommitted local changes?

6. Check if there are any files in local not pushed to GitHub.

7. Detect duplicate app structures:
- Is there any app/services code outside /expo that could cause confusion?

Final output must include:

- Sync status (FULLY SYNCED / NOT SYNCED)
- Exact missing or outdated parts
- Whether GitHub is safe to be used as source of truth

At the end:
Stop all processes.
Do not leave anything running.
